title: Karnataka government plans new Excise Act to curb illegal sale of liquor

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Nemom constituency in for a tight and unpredictable contest
content: While LDF has announced incumbent MLA V. Sivankutty as its candidate, Rajeev Chandrasekhar will contest for NDA. UDF yet to propose its candidate. With BJP  again setting high hopes for constituency, LDF fighting hard to defend its turf and UDF yet to reveal its cards, an exciting contest is on the cards in Nemom

title: There are some problems in supply of certain drugs only at primary health centres: Karnataka Health Minister
content: Acknowledging that there were some problems related to the supply of certain medicines at primary health centres in the State, Health and Family Welfare Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao, however, told the Legislative Council that the drugs were not in short supply at State-run hospitals at the district and the taluk level

title: Over 200 signatories seek Rahul Gandhi’s apology for conduct within Parliament complex
content: The signatories allege that the Congress leader and other MPs organised a protest on the steps of Parliament despite the Speaker’s direction against any such demonstration within the premises

title: Redeployment of specialist doctors from CHCs to taluk hospitals done to reduce neonatal mortality, says Karnataka Health Minister
content: Health Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao has said that redeployment of gynaecologists and anaesthetists from community health centres in rural areas with lesser deliveries to taluk hospitals was done in an effort to reduce neonatal deaths after an audit of neonatal deaths in different areas of the State

content: This forms part of the government’s objective to push for piped natural gas as it seeks to ease pressure on LPG

content: The policy, issued earlier this month, provides for mental health and counselling services, data-driven action, and a framework table for suicide prevention among students

content: The BJP will contest 89 out of the total 126 seats in the upcoming Assam assembly elections and leave 37 seats for its allies, Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma said on Tuesday.

content: Quoting studies by the International Labour Organization (ILO), the bench of Justices JB Pardiwalah and R Mahadevan noted that employed women tend to spend more time working than men once unpaid domestic labour is factored in.

title: American, Ukrainians Among 7 Arrested In India Under Anti-Terror Law
content: Seven foreign nationals including an American are in NIA custody after a Delhi court granted the anti-terror agency 11 days to investigate an alleged conspiracy to train ethnic armed groups in drone warfare. The six others are Ukrainian nationals.

content: Joseph Kent, the head of the United States' National Counterterrorism Center, has resigned from his position over the US-Israel war against Iran.

content: The High Court also asked the police to clarify why no case had been registered based on Mr. Deepak’s complaint against individuals who were clearly identifiable in the video recordings.

title: With exports halting due to Gulf war, banana farmers in Andhra Pradesh look to govt. support

content: The 59-minute programme will be broadcast on March 19 at 9.30 a.m. from the Vijayawada station and will be repeated at 10.30 a.m. on FM Rainbow 102.2., says programme head

title: No evidence of increased efficiency due to mechanisation of sewer cleaning, Centre tells LS
content: Centre says 622 deaths from hazardous cleaning reported since 2017, 842 complaints in 2025 over denial of wages, no protective gear, and caste bias; insists that sewer and septic tank cleaning work is “occupation-based”, not caste-based

title: Beyond oil & gas: How Iran war impacts India - from diamonds to fertilisers
content: Beyond oil and gas, several other sectors of the economy will likely see a hit if the US-Iran war continues for a longer duration. According to Barclays, India's non-energy imports from the Middle East are sizable as well - at approximately 10% of total non-energy imports.

title: 'I am alive': Netanyahu tells US envoy, says 'crossed off two names today'
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu humorously addressed death rumors and AI-generated claims in a new video with US Ambassador Mike Huckabee. He also highlighted recent Israeli strikes on Iranian leaders, emphasizing strong US-Israel alliance against Tehran's nuclear ambitions. This follows previous videos debunking similar misinformation.

title: Why Trump's 'beautiful armada' cannot take control of the Strait of Hormuz
content: The proposal from President Trump to assemble an international naval coalition for the defense of the Strait of Hormuz encounters formidable obstacles. Iran's advanced A2/AD capabilities—featuring an array of missiles, drones, and submarines—combined with the strait's challenging topography, complicates even the strongest navy's efforts to maintain a permanent presence.

title: Meta unit makes humans answer for AI agents; adds 13+ age rule
content: Meta's acquisition of AI agent social network Moltbook has led to significant changes in its terms of service. Users are now solely responsible for their AI agents' actions, a shift from Moltbook's previous stance. A new 13-year-old age requirement, or parental consent, has also been implemented, aligning with Meta's policies.

title: India to face Pakistan in Hockey World Cup group stage
content: India's men's and women's hockey teams have learned their group-stage opponents for the FIH Hockey World Cup, with both sides drawn in Pool D. The men will face Pakistan, England, and Wales, while the women will compete against China, England, and South Africa. Both teams will play their matches in the Netherlands.

title: Zerodha founder Nithin Kamath says: I don't use internet banking apps on my phone because ...
content: Zerodha CEO Nithin Kamath revealed he avoids banking apps due to their invasive permissions, questioning why they need access to SMS, contacts, and calls. He contrasted this with Zerodha's Kite app, which requests zero permissions, highlighting the Principle of Least Privilege and SEBI's strong two-factor authentication as enabling security without intrusive data collection.

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge is a tsunami’: Trade predicts Ranveer’s film to shatter records
content: With just days to go for the release of Dhurandhar: The Revenge, the buzz around the Ranveer Singh starrer has reached unprecedented levels. Riding high on the success of its first part, the sequel is already making waves with massive advance bookings and near housefull paid previews.

title: Popular analyst says that layoffs at Zuckerberg's Meta could trigger a 'wave of panic'
content: Meta's potential workforce reduction of up to 20% is viewed by analysts not as a setback, but as a strategic move to become an AI-first organization. This restructuring aims to leverage AI for significant cost and performance advantages, potentially creating an insurmountable competitive edge. The company's substantial AI investments and previous successes in efficiency suggest this could redefine industry standards.

title: Assam polls: NDA seat-sharing announced; BPF returns as UPPL exits
content: In a strategic political twist, the BJP has re-integrated the Bodoland People’s Front (BPF) into its NDA coalition in Assam, reinforcing its partnership with the AGP ahead of the crucial elections. This development follows the departure of the UPPL, which has chosen to run solo.

title: ‘Message can be ignored, not bullets’: Gippy Grewal receive 'threat' from Goldy Brar
content: A purported audio clip allegedly linked to foreign-based gangster Goldy Brar threatening Punjabi actor-singer Gippy Grewal surfaced on social media on Tuesday, sparking concern among fans and industry insiders. In the unverified audio recording, the caller—believed to be Brar—can be heard confronting Grewal for not responding to an earlier message, as reported by PTI.

title: Explained: Zakat al Fitr payment before Eid ul Fitr 2026 prayers
content: Ramadan 2026 brings focus to Zakat al Fitr, a vital Muslim charity. In the UAE, the amount is set at Dh25 per person or equivalent food. This obligation ensures that the less fortunate can celebrate Eid with dignity as payments are due before Eid prayers. Approved channels facilitate transparent distribution.  This tradition highlights compassion and collective care.

title: Lions emerge as surprise destination for Cameron Jordan in NFL trade buzz
content: Cameron Jordan still wants to feel valued, and that has put his Saints future in real doubt. After a 10.5-sack season in 2025, the veteran edge rusher remains unsigned. The Detroit Lions have been floated as a possible fit, but New Orleans is not out yet. Here’s why this rumor has real traction and what it means now.

title: J.J. McCarthy linked to shock move as ex-GM suggests Vikings exit
content: The Vikings’ signing of Kyler Murray has sparked growing uncertainty around J.J. McCarthy’s future, turning him into a potential trade candidate. Mike Tannenbaum’s suggestion of a reunion with Jim Harbaugh and the Chargers adds intrigue, though questions remain about fit and role behind Justin Herbert. For now, Minnesota appears more likely to let the situation play out, but the pressure to make a clear decision is steadily increasing.

title: Cardi B reveals hospitalization after cheating, Diggs attempts romance
content: Stefon Diggs, the popular wide receiver in the league, is facing a tough time in his career after the New England Patriots released him recently, as fans continue to troll him for allegedly cheating on Cardi B even as she gave birth to their child. Amid all the mess, Cardi B's statements about being cheated on have resurfaced and sparked panic.

title: Middle East crisis: Can UAE employer stop salary if one's stranded outside?
content: Geopolitical tensions have left many expatriates stranded abroad, raising questions about salary continuation. UAE labour law dictates salary is tied to work but unavoidable absences due to conflict or travel bans are considered justified. Outcomes depend on remote work capability, employer agreement and clear communication, with flexibility and negotiation being key.

title: Rohit Sharma gets Mumbai Indians head coach's boost ahead of IPL 2026
content: Mumbai Indians head coach Mahela Jayawardene praised Mumbai Indians’ strong core, with Suryakumar Yadav, Jasprit Bumrah, Hardik Pandya and Tilak Varma shining in India’s T20 World Cup win. MI have given them an extended break. He backed Suryakumar, praised Bumrah, hinted at a bigger role for Rohit Sharma, and welcomed healthy selection competition.

title: 'Good thing he's out': Trump after counterterror chief quits over Iran war
content: US President Trump welcomed the resignation of NCTC director Joe Kent, calling him "very weak on security" after Kent protested the Iran war. Kent stated Iran posed no imminent threat, attributing the conflict to Israeli pressure. Republicans defended the strikes, while Democrats sided with Kent, highlighting internal dissent amid heightened domestic terror concerns.

title: 'Weren’t fussed' claim was tough to take: England's Ollie Pope on Ashes criticism
content: England, led by Ben Stokes, lost the 2025/26 Ashes series 4-1 to Australia cricket team. The tour is still under review by the England and Wales Cricket Board.

title: Horoscope Today: Daily astrological predictions for March 18
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for March 18, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Middle East war: Elastic prices to rise 25%, to hit undergarment business
content: Tirupur, a major hub for undergarment manufacturing with an annual turnover of around Rs 10,000 crore in both export and domestic markets, depends heavily on elastic as a key accessory.

title: 'We don't need help!' Trump explodes after Nato, other nations refuse to take part in Iran war
content: US President Donald Trump declared America's self-sufficiency in its military operations against Iran, stating the nation is the world's most powerful and requires no allied support. He noted that many Nato allies declined to join the campaign, which he views as a one-way street. Trump also claimed significant military successes against Iran.

title: 'So it’s official?' Fans react as Lewis Hamilton comments on Kim Kardashian’s post
content: Romance rumours between seven-time world champion Lewis Hamilton and reality TV star Kim Kardashian intensified after his reaction to her Instagram post sparked fan buzz. The duo was recently spotted together as well. Meanwhile, Hamilton returned to the podium at the Chinese Grand Prix, securing his first top-three finish with Ferrari.

title: Ex-world no. 1 Caroline Wozniacki shares video of kids Olivia playing Tennis
content: Caroline Wozniacki has given fans a glimpse into her life off the court, and it is quickly becoming a viral tennis moment, with fans loving the adorable, next generation energy.

title: 'I am sorry': How Suryakumar Yadav defused Arshdeep Singh-Daryl Mitchell scuffle
content: India T20 captain Suryakumar Yadav apologised to Daryl Mitchell after Arshdeep Singh accidentally hit him with a throw in the World Cup final. Arshdeep was fined but later apologised. Suryakumar said such moments happen in heat but stressed leadership and sportsmanship, adding he said sorry regardless of intent to set the right example.

title: 'Kick' bans 'Ac7ionMan' as viral video fuels relapse rumors, streamer responds
content: Ac7ionMan has been banned from Kick after a viral video led to claims of a fentanyl relapse. The streamer denied the claim, saying he took an over-the-counter pill without knowing its strength. His channel was removed the same day, and he later confirmed a 14-day ban while trying to get it reduced.

title: 'Iran posed no threat': Trump's counterterror chief resigns, cites Israel 'pressure'
content: A top counterterrorism official, Joe Kent, resigned, stating Iran posed no imminent threat and the US-Iran conflict was driven by Israeli pressure and misinformation. Kent, who served as director of the National Counterterrorism Center (NCTC), announced his decision saying, after much reflection, I have decided to resign from my position effective today.

title: 'Don't play just for match-ups': Ex-coach wants this PBKS bowler to play 'every match'
content: Former India cricketer Sanjay Bangar advocates for Harpreet Brar's inclusion in every Punjab Kings match this season, citing his effectiveness against all batters and ability to provide crucial powerplay overs. Bangar also expressed confidence in the opening partnership of Prabhsimran Singh and Priyansh Arya, though he noted Arya's recent domestic form could be a factor.

title: Passive euthanasia: Harish Rana case revived Aruna Shanbaug’s long fight for dignity in death
content: The Supreme Court has granted approval for passive euthanasia for Harish Rana, a 32-year-old man in a vegetative state for over 13 years. This historic verdict allows the withdrawal of life support, offering a dignified death and ending his family's prolonged agony. The decision follows recommendations from medical boards and personal interaction with Rana's parents.

title: Argentina formally exits WHO, following US withdrawal earlier this year
content: Argentina has officially exited the World Health Organization, following a year-long announcement by President Javier Milei's administration. Citing the WHO's alleged mishandling of the Covid-19 pandemic and political motivations over science, Buenos Aires has completed its withdrawal. This move mirrors the United States' earlier departure and raises concerns about global health coordination.

title: Over $100mn per day: How global oil shock has turned into a jackpot for Iran and Russia
content: A US-Iran war is causing a dangerous oil shock, ironically enriching both Tehran and Moscow. As Gulf energy assets and shipping are disrupted, crude prices surge, providing a revenue windfall for Iran and benefiting Russia through tighter global supply and renewed Asian demand.

title: Nana Patekar says only Amitabh Bachchan and he call actors directly
content: Veteran actor Nana Patekar and director Prakash Jha have voiced concerns about the growing complexity and cost associated with working with big stars. They highlighted how unreachable stars and their large entourages, managed by corporate structures, inflate film budgets, making direct communication and creative collaboration increasingly difficult.

title: Matosevic handed four-year suspension after doping admission, ITIA acts
content: Marinko Matosevic has been banned for four years after being found guilty of multiple doping violations. The International Tennis Integrity Agency confirmed the decision, which also includes returning prize money. While Matosevic admitted to some actions and criticised the process, the tribunal rejected his claims and called his conduct a serious breach of tennis rules.

title: Iran vs US tensions cloud FIFA World Cup plans, matches may shift to Mexico
content: Iran’s 2026 World Cup participation is uncertain amid rising tensions. The country is negotiating with FIFA to shift matches from the US to Mexico, citing safety concerns. Officials said, "We will certainly not travel to America." FIFA hasn’t agreed yet. After Donald Trump warned about safety, Iran insisted "no one can exclude" them from the tournament.

title: Exclusive - 'Action will follow. Pak's brutality won't go unanswered': Taliban
content: Taliban spokesperson Zabihullah Mujahid vowed retaliation after an alleged Pakistani airstrike on Omar Addiction Treatment Hospital in Kabul, which he described as a deliberate attack on civilians. Mujahid stated the attack "will not go unanswered" and accused Pakistan of attempting to shape the narrative, vowing to expose their "brutality" to global human rights bodies.

title: AB de Villiers tells MS Dhoni 'bat higher or be out of CSK squad'
content: Dhoni, 44, will play for CSK when the season begins on March 28. De Villiers said Dhoni’s recent trend of batting at No. 8 or 9 limits his impact.
